title: Razorpay Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis
text: Razorpay Domestic Pricing (as of 2024): Standard MDR for credit cards: 2% per transaction. Debit cards: 0.40% for transactions below ₹2,000, 0.75% above ₹2,000. UPI: 0% (zero MDR per NPCI mandate). Net banking: ₹7 flat per transaction. Wallets (Paytm, PhonePe): 1.5% per transaction. EMI on cards: 1.5% per transaction. Enterprise clients with annual volume above ₹500 crore can negotiate rates down to 1.4-1.65% on cards. Razorpay charges no setup fee and no monthly fee. Settlement: T+1 for UPI and T+2 for cards by default. Instant settlement available at 0.25% additional charge. International payments: Razorpay supports 100+ currencies. International MDR: 3% + currency conversion at standard bank rates. Chargeback fee: ₹350 per disputed transaction. Refund: Full refund free, partial refund free. Razorpay Route (split payments/marketplace): 0.25% routing fee. Subscriptions/recurring: No extra charge. Razorpay Capital (lending): Separate product, no impact on MDR.

title: Stripe Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis
text: Stripe Standard Pricing (as of 2024): All cards (domestic and international): 2.9% + $0.30 per transaction. This is a flat rate with no negotiation for standard accounts. Enterprise pricing available for volume above $1M/month — negotiated rates typically 2.2-2.5% + $0.25. International card surcharge: +1.5% additional for cards issued outside your country. Currency conversion: 1% fee if currency conversion required. Stripe does not support UPI natively — significant disadvantage for India-first businesses. ACH bank transfers (US): 0.8% capped at $5. SEPA Direct Debit (Europe): 0.8% capped at €5. Stripe Billing (subscriptions): Included at no extra cost for standard plans. Stripe Connect (marketplace): 0.25% + standard processing fees. Dispute/chargeback fee: $15 per dispute. Refunds: Processing fees not returned on refunds — this is a key difference from Razorpay. Stripe Radar (fraud): Included in standard pricing. Stripe Terminal (in-person): 2.7% + $0.05 per transaction. Settlement: 2 business days (US), 7 business days for new accounts.

title: PayU India Pricing and Fee Structure - Internal Analysis
text: PayU India Pricing (as of 2024): Credit cards: 1.99% per transaction. Debit cards: 0.40% below ₹2,000, 0.75% above ₹2,000 (same as RBI mandate). UPI: 0% (NPCI mandate). Net banking: ₹6 per transaction. EMI: 1.5%. International: 3% + $0.30. PayU enterprise accounts (above ₹200 crore annual volume) can negotiate 1.55-1.80% on cards. PayU is part of Prosus group. Settlement: T+2 standard, T+1 available on request. PayU known for strong presence in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities. PayU LazyPay (BNPL): Available, separate agreement. PayU Payout (bulk disbursement): ₹3-5 per transaction flat. Dispute management portal less sophisticated than Razorpay or Stripe. Customer support: Phone and email, response time 24-48 hours.

title: Razorpay vs Stripe - Head to Head Comparison for Enterprise Clients
text: Razorpay vs Stripe - Key Differences for Enterprise Decision Making. Use Razorpay when: 90%+ transactions are domestic India. Need UPI support (0% MDR advantage). Need NACH/e-mandate for subscriptions. Require Indian regulatory compliance out of the box. Need vernacular language support in checkout. Have volume above ₹100 crore for negotiated rates. Use Stripe when: More than 40% revenue is international. Need multi-currency billing (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.). Serving US or European customers primarily. Need advanced developer APIs and webhooks. Building a global SaaS product. Cost comparison example: ₹10 lakh domestic transaction via Razorpay at 1.75% = ₹17,500. Same via Stripe at 2.9% = ₹29,000. Razorpay saves ₹11,500 per ₹10 lakh in domestic volume. For international transactions: Razorpay charges 3%, Stripe charges 2.9% + 1.5% international surcharge = 4.4%. Razorpay is cheaper internationally too. Verdict for pure Indian businesses: Razorpay wins on cost. Verdict for international SaaS: Stripe wins on coverage and features. Hybrid strategy recommendation: Use Razorpay for India billing, Stripe for international billing.

title: CCAvenue Pricing and Overview
text: CCAvenue (Infibeam Avenues) Pricing 2024: Credit cards: 2% per transaction. Debit cards: 1.25% per transaction (higher than competitor mandated rates — legacy issue). Net banking: ₹7 per transaction. UPI: 0% (mandate). International: 3.5% per transaction. CCAvenue has 200+ payment options, one of the widest in India. Strong in government, education, and utility payments. Older technology stack compared to Razorpay. Dashboard and reporting less sophisticated. Settlement: T+3 standard. CCAvenue Xtend (EMI): 1.75%. Enterprise rates negotiable above ₹50 crore annual volume. Key weakness: Debit card MDR is above market rate. Customer support: Average response time 48-72 hours. Best suited for: Government portals, educational institutions, traditional businesses not needing modern API integration.

title: International Payment Processing - Deep Dive
text: International Payment Processing Options for Indian Companies 2024. For Indian companies accepting payments from international customers: Razorpay International: Accepts 100+ currencies. Settles in INR. MDR: 3% flat. No international surcharge. Requires FEMA compliance and AD bank tie-up. Good for companies doing below $500K annual international volume. Stripe (with Indian entity): Accepts 135+ currencies. Settles in INR, USD, EUR. MDR: 2.9% + $0.30 + 1.5% international surcharge (total ~4.4% for cross-border). Better developer experience. Required for companies with heavy US/EU customer base. PayPal (for international): MDR: 3.49% + $0.49. High fees but high consumer trust in US. Useful for B2C international. Cashfree International: 3% + $0.30. Emerging option. OPGSP (Overseas Payment Service Provider) model: Indian companies can accept international cards through Razorpay/Stripe acting as OPGSP. Regulatory note: All international payments must be reported to AD Bank within 7 days under FEMA. GST not applicable on international transactions (zero-rated exports). Key decision factor: If more than 30% of your revenue is international, evaluate Stripe. Below 30%, Razorpay international is sufficient and cheaper.

title: Payment Processor Reliability and Uptime Comparison 2024
text: Uptime and Reliability Data for Major Payment Processors in India (2024): Razorpay: 99.97% uptime in 2023. 2 major incidents: Oct 2023 UPI outage (45 mins), Jan 2024 netbanking slowdown (2 hours). Mean time to resolve (MTTR): 38 minutes. Stripe: 99.99% uptime globally. India-specific incidents: March 2024 Indian card authentication delay (30 mins). MTTR: 22 minutes. PayU: 99.93% uptime. Multiple incidents in 2023 including a 4-hour settlement processing delay in Q3. MTTR: 92 minutes — highest among top processors. CCAvenue: 99.88% uptime. Known for periodic slowdowns during peak festival season (Diwali). Razorpay Smart Routing: Automatically retries failed transactions on alternate bank routes — effectively increases success rate by 3-4%. Recommended SLA to include in contracts: Minimum 99.95% uptime guarantee, ₹X penalty per hour of downtime beyond SLA. Current clients with SLA clauses: Meesho, CRED, Groww, Swiggy Instamart.

title: Payment Processor Settlement Timelines - Client Impact Analysis
text: Settlement Timeline Comparison and Business Impact (2024). Standard settlement timelines: Razorpay: T+1 for UPI, T+2 for cards. Instant settlement (add 0.25% fee). Stripe: T+2 for established accounts, T+7 for new accounts. Payouts on Friday settle Monday. PayU: T+2 standard, T+1 available for premium accounts. CCAvenue: T+3 standard, T+1 available at extra cost. Business impact of settlement delay: A client with ₹5 crore daily GMV at T+2 settlement = ₹10 crore permanently locked in float. At 8% cost of capital, this is ₹80 lakh annual opportunity cost. Instant settlement at 0.25% cost: For ₹5 crore daily volume = ₹1.25 lakh daily charge = ₹4.5 crore annual. Not worth it for most businesses — float opportunity cost is lower. Recommendation: Negotiate T+1 standard settlement (without extra charge) for accounts above ₹100 crore annual volume. Current clients on T+1: CRED, Meesho, PharmEasy, Groww. Clients with settlement complaints: Juspay (T+3 delay in Q1 2024), Dunzo (wants T+1 but on Stripe standard which is T+2).

title: UPI and RuPay - Zero MDR Impact on Business
text: UPI Zero MDR — Business Implications 2024. NPCI Mandate: Since January 2020, UPI transactions carry 0% MDR for merchants. This is a government mandate. No payment processor charges MDR on UPI regardless of volume. UPI market share in India: 80% of all digital transactions by volume (2024). Average UPI transaction size: ₹1,200. Impact on payment processor revenue: Razorpay, PayU, and others earn nothing on UPI transactions. They earn only on cards, netbanking, EMI, and wallets. Strategic implication for clients: If your transaction mix is 70%+ UPI, your blended MDR is very low. Example: Meesho — 75% UPI volume → blended MDR of 0.38% despite 1.75% card rate. Zepto — 80% UPI volume → blended MDR of 0.36%. RuPay cards: Also 0% MDR since 2019. Premium RuPay credit cards: 0% MDR for merchants. CBDC (Digital Rupee): Pilot stage, 0% MDR expected. Implication for processor choice: For high-UPI businesses, processor MDR differences matter less. Focus on reliability, settlement speed, and value-added services instead.

title: Chargeback and Dispute Management - Processor Comparison
text: Chargeback and Dispute Management Comparison 2024. Chargeback rates by industry (India averages): E-commerce: 0.6-0.8%. Subscription SaaS: 0.3-0.5%. Food delivery: 0.2-0.4%. Travel: 1.2-1.8%. Chargeback fee comparison: Razorpay: ₹350 per dispute, regardless of outcome. Stripe: $15 (approx ₹1,250) per dispute, refunded if merchant wins. PayU: ₹250 per dispute. CCAvenue: ₹400 per dispute. Dispute resolution time: Razorpay: Average 15 business days. Stripe: Average 75-90 days (US timeline applies). PayU: Average 21 business days. Razorpay advantage: Disputes resolved under Indian banking rules — faster and more predictable. Stripe advantage: Stripe Radar AI fraud detection reduces disputes by 25% proactively. Current clients with high chargeback rates: Globex Technologies (international cards = high dispute rate), Dunzo (delivery disputes). Recommendation: High-dispute clients should use Razorpay for lower per-dispute cost and faster resolution.

title: Internal Sales Playbook - Payment Processor Objection Handling
text: Common Objections and Responses for Payment Processor Comparisons. Objection 1: 'Stripe has better developer experience.' Response: Razorpay APIs are equally well-documented and specifically optimized for Indian payment rails. Razorpay has SDKs for React, Android, iOS, Flutter. Razorpay Checkout is a single line of code. Developer NPS for Razorpay in India is 68 vs Stripe's global NPS of 72 — nearly equivalent. Objection 2: 'Stripe supports more countries.' Response: True for pure international play. But Razorpay covers 100+ currencies for acceptance. If your business is India-first with some international, Razorpay is superior. Only choose Stripe if 40%+ revenue is from outside India. Objection 3: 'Our current processor is good enough.' Response: Calculate their current blended MDR and compare to what Razorpay can offer. For a ₹100 crore annual volume business, a 0.3% MDR reduction saves ₹30 lakh per year. Objection 4: 'Switching payment processors is too risky.' Response: Razorpay migration team handles full switchover. Zero downtime migration plan. Parallel run for 2 weeks to validate. Reference: Dunzo migrated from PayU to Razorpay in 3 weeks with zero downtime in 2022. Objection 5: 'We are already on Stripe and everything works.' Response: Fair. For international SaaS, Stripe is the right choice. Only push Razorpay if they have significant domestic volume being processed at 2.9%.

title: At-Risk Clients - Q2 2024 Review
text: At-Risk Client Review - Q2 2024. Priority 1 (High Churn Risk): Dunzo Daily — Health Score 55/100. Primary concern: MDR too high on Stripe at 2.9% vs Razorpay domestic rates. Action required: Immediate pricing proposal from Karthik. Deadline: Before September renewal. Juspay India — Health Score 68/100. Primary concern: Settlement delays Q1 2024 unresolved. Action required: Engineering escalation, SLA compensation offer. Groww — Health Score 72/100. Primary concern: NACH mandate rejection rate 6% vs industry 3.5%. Action required: Technical review with Groww engineering. Priority 2 (Watch): Globex Technologies — Health Score 74/100. Concern: Evaluating Razorpay for domestic leg. Risk: Partial revenue loss, not full churn. PharmEasy — Health Score 78/100. Concern: DPDP Act compliance uncertainty. Healthy accounts (no action needed): CRED (96), Postman (94), Nykaa (91), ClearTax (89), Zepto (88).

title: BNPL and Credit Line Products - Processor Comparison
text: Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) Options through Payment Processors 2024. Razorpay BNPL Partners: Simpl, ZestMoney, LazyPay, Flexmoney. MDR on BNPL: 2.0-2.5% merchant fee, higher than standard card MDR. Razorpay manages all BNPL partners through single integration. Stripe: No native BNPL in India. Klarna and Afterpay not available in Indian market. Merchants must integrate BNPL separately outside Stripe. PayU LazyPay: PayU's own BNPL product. 2.5% merchant fee. 3.5 crore active users. Strong in Tier-2 cities. Conversion impact of BNPL: Adding BNPL typically increases checkout conversion by 12-18% for average ticket sizes above ₹1,500. Current clients using BNPL: Nykaa Fashion (via Razorpay — all major BNPL partners), Meesho (Simpl integration via Razorpay). Clients who should consider BNPL: PharmEasy (average ₹680 ticket — borderline), UrbanCompany (₹1,100 average — strong candidate). BNPL regulatory risk: RBI has tightened BNPL regulations in 2023. Ensure clients are aware that BNPL lenders must be RBI-registered NBFCs.

title: GST and Tax Compliance on Payment Processing Fees
text: GST on Payment Processing Fees 2024. Payment gateway service fees are subject to 18% GST in India. This is charged on top of the MDR. Example: Razorpay charges 1.75% MDR + 18% GST on that fee. Effective cost to merchant: 1.75% * 1.18 = 2.065% of transaction value. Stripe charges 2.9% MDR + 18% GST. Effective cost: 2.9% * 1.18 = 3.422%. GST credit: If the merchant is GST-registered (B2B), they can claim input tax credit (ITC) on the GST paid to the payment processor. This effectively reduces the net cost. For a GST-registered enterprise client: Net effective MDR after ITC = base MDR only (GST neutralized). For B2C merchants (not GST registered): Full 2.065% or 3.422% is the actual cost. Implication: For enterprise clients (all B2B, GST-registered), the GST component of MDR is a pass-through. The actual comparison should be on base MDR only: Razorpay 1.75% vs Stripe 2.9% — net of GST. Compliance: Payment processors must issue GST invoices monthly. All our processors are compliant.

title: Payment Processor Integration and Technical Evaluation
text: Technical Integration Comparison 2024. API Quality: Razorpay: REST API, well-documented, India-specific (UPI, NACH, QR). Stripe: REST API, industry gold standard, better webhook reliability, superior testing tools (Stripe CLI). PayU: REST API, older design, less consistent error responses. Integration time estimate: Razorpay basic checkout: 1-2 days. Razorpay full integration (subscriptions, payouts, webhooks): 1-2 weeks. Stripe basic: 2-3 days (more complex initial setup). Stripe full: 2-3 weeks. Mobile SDK quality: Razorpay Android/iOS SDK — excellent, 4.6/5 Play Store. Stripe Mobile SDK — excellent, 4.7/5 Play Store. Webhook reliability: Stripe webhooks: 99.98% delivery, automatic retry, full event log. Razorpay webhooks: 99.90% delivery, manual retry required on failure. This gap matters for subscription billing and payout automation. PCI DSS Compliance: All major processors (Razorpay, Stripe, PayU) are PCI DSS Level 1 compliant. Merchants using hosted checkout are automatically compliant. Testing environment: Stripe test mode is superior — all card scenarios, simulated failures, full parity with production. Razorpay sandbox is adequate but has gaps (NACH testing limited).
